claim: Contaminated unlabeled logs hurt naive BC
evidence: PointNav all-data BC drops from 0.460 to 0.153 as bad fraction rises from 50% to 95%; Lift MG all-demo reaches 0.200 at 20k; Can MG all-demo reaches 0.100 at 20k
caveat: Requires strong backbone for robotics interpretation

claim: Scarce positive and bad labels can recover useful support
evidence: PointNav score-gap demo BC gets 1.000 success across 50/75/90/95% bad; Can balanced support has 0.962 aggregate purity and 0.900 success at 20k; Square and Transport relative-quality labels recover high-purity support on new task families
caveat: Robotics rows validate support selection plus BC-RNN-GMM
source: not full inverse-Q; Square/Transport are support-side relative-quality diagnostics

claim: Purity and coverage must be adapted
evidence: Balanced Can favors coverage-gap; heavy 20p/80b favors top20 precision; 40p/80b favors mass-capped coverage at 15k/20k
caveat: Needs an explicit algorithmic converter in the final method

claim: Hard support can beat weighted BC at fixed later checkpoints
evidence: Can 40p/80b masscap reaches 0.733 versus weighted 0.567 at 20k, and remains ahead in a three-seed 50-episode endpoint check: 0.680 versus 0.573; Lift pos-min reaches 0.667 versus weighted 0.533 at 20k
caveat: Weighted BC is competitive and sometimes better earlier; the higher-episode Can endpoint gap is modest and approximate intervals overlap

claim: Soft weighted BC can be better when broad coverage matters
evidence: Can MG weighted sampler beats matched all-positive, all-demo, and pos-p10 controls at fixed 20k: 0.333 versus 0.200, 0.100, and 0.167
caveat: Stress diagnostic only; absolute success is modest and the hard-soft converter is not final

evidence: Positive-only NN top40 is a strong Can 40p/80b control and ties masscap on best-per-seed mean; Lift positive-only NN top160 is viable but remains below bad-aware pos-min at fixed 15k/20k and oracle-by-seed mean
caveat: Claim stability and calibration from bad labels instead

claim: Full Tri-PIQL inverse-Q actor extraction beats all real-data baselines
evidence: Minari and early feed-forward/GMM real-data screens were negative or unstable
caveat: Current positive robotics evidence is selector or weighted sampling plus official BC-RNN-GMM
